summary: Design complete Supabase schema + RLS + auth model for قاف multi-tenant SaaS, then adversarial review

design_notes: Multi-tenant isolation enforced at DB layer: every tenant table has tenant_id uuid NOT NULL with RLS policy `tenant_id = (SELECT tenant_id FROM public.users WHERE id = auth.uid())`. Service role bypasses RLS for platform operations and webhooks.

public.users extends auth.users via id = auth.users.id (1:1). Trigger on auth.users insert creates corresponding public.users row. Role + tenant_id + status live here; JWT custom claims are populated from this table via a Supabase auth hook to avoid recursive RLS lookups in hot paths.

Feature-flag gating: addon-gated tables include an RLS clause `EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM tenant_addons ta WHERE ta.tenant_id = X AND ta.feature_key = 'KEY' AND ta.status='active')`. Tenant without addon sees empty result, never an error — meeting the non-negotiable.

Soft-delete: user-facing tables use deleted_at timestamptz; RLS hides rows where deleted_at IS NOT NULL except for admins/auditors. A nightly cron purges rows past 30 days for hard delete (GDPR right-to-erasure compliant).

Audit log captures actor_id, tenant_id, entity_type, entity_id, action, before/after JSONB snapshots, ip, user_agent. Sensitive tables (cases, contracts, invoices, salaries, wallets) write via triggers.
Subdomain routing: tenants.subdomain is unique and lowercase-validated. Edge function resolves tenant_id from Host header before queries.

Moyasar integration: payments table stores moyasar_payment_id, moyasar_invoice_id; subscriptions store moyasar_subscription_id and moyasar_customer_id. Webhook handler runs under service_role and updates subscription status atomically.
Trial logic: subscriptions.trial_ends_at = created_at + 14 days; status enum includes 'trialing'. Cron flips to 'past_due' when expired without payment.
EU/Frankfurt residency: Supabase project region eu-central-1; all storage buckets and DB physically in Frankfurt; no cross-region replication for tenant data.
JSONB used only for: address blobs, metadata bags, audit before/after snapshots, webhook payloads, notification payload. NOT used for queryable core entities (cases, invoices, etc are normalized).

Indexes: every tenant table has (tenant_id, created_at DESC) compound for list views. FK columns indexed. Partial indexes WHERE deleted_at IS NULL for hot paths. GIN indexes on full-text search columns (cases.title_ar, memos.body_ar, regulations.text_ar) using arabic config.
Storage buckets all private; paths prefixed with tenant_id/ so RLS on storage.objects validates `(storage.foldername(name))[1]::uuid = (SELECT tenant_id FROM public.users WHERE id = auth.uid())`.

Wallets: every employee gets a wallet auto-created on user insert via trigger. wallet_transactions is append-only (no UPDATE/DELETE policy except for service_role). Balance computed from sum or cached on wallets.balance with trigger.
Commissions: BD/marketer commissions computed from invoices.paid_amount * commission_rate, written to wallet_transactions with type='commission_credit'.
Capacity entries: weekly self-reports of hours-available per lawyer used by manager dashboard for case assignment fairness.
Internal regulations: firm-specific HR/policy docs separate from public legal regulations (Saudi laws). Both modeled but distinct tables.

Tasks vs Requests vs Notifications: tasks are work items (assigned, due, status); requests are formal internal requests (leave, expense reimbursement, document signature) with approval flow; notifications are ephemeral UI alerts.
Case timeline: append-only event log per case (status change, document added, hearing scheduled, party added). Drives the activity feed UI.
Memo history: every memo edit creates a memo_history row with full body snapshot for legal auditability.
Schedule events covers hearings, meetings, deadlines — single table with event_type enum and optional case_id link.

Platform admin separation: platform_admins table is OUTSIDE tenant scope; auth.users for platform admins have NULL tenant_id and role='platform_admin'. RLS on platform tables checks `EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM platform_admins WHERE user_id = auth.uid())`.
rls_principles: Default DENY: every table has RLS ENABLED with no permissive policy until explicitly granted. Service role bypasses RLS for webhooks, cron, and platform operations.
Tenant isolation expression (canonical): `tenant_id = (SELECT tenant_id FROM public.users WHERE id = auth.uid())`. Used in USING and WITH CHECK for SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE.

Role-based action gating: write policies check `(SELECT role FROM public.users WHERE id = auth.uid()) IN (...)`. Lawyers cannot edit invoices; accountants cannot edit memos; only admin/general_manager/executive_director can change roles.

Addon-gated tables (consulting_hours, weekly_reports, capacity_entries, advanced_intel views): policy adds `EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM tenant_addons WHERE tenant_id = (SELECT tenant_id FROM public.users WHERE id = auth.uid()) AND feature_key = '<KEY>' AND status = 'active' AND (expires_at IS NULL OR expires_at > now()))`. Without addon: empty result, no error.
Soft-delete visibility: SELECT policies on user-facing tables include `AND deleted_at IS NULL` unless caller role IN ('admin','auditor','general_manager').
Append-only tables (wallet_transactions, audit_log, case_timeline, memo_history): no UPDATE or DELETE policy granted — only INSERT + SELECT.
Self-only data (notifications, attendance own row, requests as requester): policy `user_id = auth.uid()` in addition to tenant isolation.

Platform tables (tenants, subscriptions, payments, platform_admins): RLS allows only platform_admins via `EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM platform_admins WHERE user_id = auth.uid())`; tenant's own SELECT on their tenants row allowed via id match.
Storage RLS: bucket policies check `(storage.foldername(name))[1]::uuid = (SELECT tenant_id FROM public.users WHERE id = auth.uid())` so file paths must begin with the tenant_id folder.

JWT claim cache: optional optimization — Supabase auth hook injects tenant_id + role into JWT app_metadata; RLS expressions can use `auth.jwt() ->> 'tenant_id'` instead of subquery for hot paths. Both forms kept equivalent.
dbSchema.indexes_strategy: "Every tenant-scoped table has a compound (tenant_id, created_at DESC) for paginated list views and (tenant_id, deleted_at) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL for active-row filtering. Every foreign key column is indexed to keep deletes and joins fast. Status enums used in dashboards get partial indexes like (tenant_id, status) WHERE status IN ('open','in_progress'). Full-text search uses GIN indexes with `to_tsvector('arabic', col)` on cases.title_ar+description_ar, memos.body_ar, regulations.text_ar, templates.body_ar, precedents.text_ar. Date-range queries (schedule_events.starts_at, invoices.due_date, licenses.expires_at) get BRIN indexes for append-heavy tables and BTREE for selective queries. Hash indexes avoided in favor of BTREE. Unique constraints: tenants.subdomain, users(tenant_id,email), invoice_number(tenant_id,number), case_number(tenant_id,number). JSONB metadata columns get GIN indexes only where queried (audit_log.changes, notifications.payload)."

authModel.auth_provider: Supabase Auth (GoTrue) — single global auth.users pool, NOT per-tenant. Tenant binding lives in public.tenant_users (junction). Email+password primary; Microsoft/Google OAuth optional per tenant. SMS OTP NOT used (cost/Saudi telco). Hosted on the same eu-central-1 (Frankfurt) Supabase project — no separate auth project, no federated SSO in v1.

jwt_claims.purpose: Stable user identity used as FK from public.tenant_users.user_id and in RLS via auth.uid().
Display + invite redemption matching; never used directly in RLS.

jwt_claims.source: Supabase Auth (built-in) — Authenticator Assurance Level: aal1 (password) or aal2 (after TOTP).
jwt_claims.purpose: RLS policies on sensitive tables (financial, audit, MFA-required roles) check (auth.jwt()->>'aal') = 'aal2'.

jwt_claims.source: custom_access_token_hook (Postgres function public.qaf_access_token_hook) — looks up the user's single active row in public.tenant_users (where status='active' and revoked_at is null) and injects tenant_id.
jwt_claims.purpose: PRIMARY isolation key. Every RLS policy reads (auth.jwt()->>'tenant_id')::uuid and compares to row.tenant_id. If the user has zero active memberships the hook injects tenant_id=null so every tenant policy fails closed.

jwt_claims.source: custom_access_token_hook — reads tenant_users.role (the 11-value enum: admin, general_manager, executive_director, partner, manager, lawyer, consultant, marketer, auditor, accountant, secretary).
jwt_claims.purpose: Drives row-level + column-level policies and UI gating. Encoded as text claim 'qaf_role' to avoid clashing with PostgREST's reserved 'role' claim.

jwt_claims.source: custom_access_token_hook — joins tenant_users → tenants → subscriptions (Moyasar-synced) and reads the active tier: trial | base49 | tier199 | tier499 | tier1999 | suspended.
jwt_claims.purpose: Cheap tier check in RLS and Edge Functions without re-querying. Refreshed on every access token issue (~1h).

jwt_claims.source: custom_access_token_hook — array_agg of subscription_addons.addon_code where active=true for the tenant. Always an array (possibly empty).
jwt_claims.purpose: Feature-flag check inside RLS: an addon-gated table policy adds AND 'bd_module' = ANY (auth.jwt()->'enabled_addons'::text[]). When the addon is not enabled the policy fails and SELECT returns empty rows, never an error — per the non-negotiables.

jwt_claims.source: custom_access_token_hook — tenants.status (active | trial | past_due | suspended | deleted).
jwt_claims.purpose: Suspended/deleted short-circuits all RLS via a single guard policy: status IN ('active','trial','past_due') — past_due still reads but blocks writes.

jwt_claims.source: custom_access_token_hook — monotonically increasing int tenant_users.membership_version, bumped on role change / revocation.
jwt_claims.purpose: Lets the refresh-token denylist invalidate stale sessions cleanly without nuking other devices.

jwt_claims.source: Supabase Auth fixed — 'authenticated' for logged-in, 'anon' for anonymous.
jwt_claims.purpose: Postgres GRANTs are scoped to these two roles only; the 11 app roles do NOT map to Postgres roles.

jwt_claims.source: Set by service-role on auth.users when seeding the qaf.sa platform owner. Hook reads it and emits 'is_platform_admin: true'.
jwt_claims.purpose: Single short-circuit for admin.qaf.sa — a separate set of policies on platform tables. Tenant tables still require tenant_id, so platform admin reading tenant data must impersonate via a logged service-role RPC, not via JWT.

authModel.session_strategy: @supabase/ssr in Next 16 App Router. createServerClient in a server-only helper (lib/supabase/server.ts) reads sb-access-token + sb-refresh-token from httpOnly, Secure, SameSite=Lax cookies scoped to .qaf.sa so subdomain tenants share the auth session, but RLS still isolates by tenant_id claim. middleware.ts runs on every request: (1) parses subdomain → resolves tenant slug → looks up tenants.id, (2) calls supabase.auth.getUser() which validates the JWT against GoTrue (no trust of the cookie alone), (3) compares jwt.tenant_id to the URL subdomain — mismatch = 404 (not 403, to avoid tenant enumeration), (4) if tenant_status='suspended' redirect to /suspended, (5) if path is in MFA_REQUIRED_PATHS and aal!='aal2' redirect to /mfa. Access token TTL 1h, refresh token 30d sliding. Refresh tokens are rotated on every use (Supabase default). Server Components use cookies()-bound client (RLS = user JWT). Server Actions same. Route Handlers same. The browser client (createBrowserClient) is only used for realtime subscriptions and never for mutations of sensitive data. NO localStorage tokens — cookies only, so XSS can't exfiltrate the session.

can_do: Assign any role except admin transfer (separate flow)
cannot_do: Bypass MFA — admin role is MFA-required at aal2
Hard-delete tenant data without 30-day soft-delete window unless GDPR-erasure flow
Access raw client PII without case assignment

can_do: Full read/write on assigned cases + own client portfolio
Read all firm cases (read-only for non-assigned)
cannot_do: Bypass MFA — partner is MFA-required at aal2

can_do: Read assigned cases (no edit on legal strategy)
Full BD pipeline access (if 'bd_module' addon enabled)
Read aggregated case-type stats (no client PII)
cannot_do: Operates on empty result if BD addon disabled
ANY write (RLS denies INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE for auditor)

can_do: Full finance module (invoices, payments, expenses, payroll)
Read case billing metadata (no legal content)
cannot_do: Read audit log entries unrelated to finance

can_do: Read case header data (name, client, status) for cases their assigned lawyers own

authModel.invite_flow:
1) Admin (or general_manager with delegated permission) on tenant.qaf.sa opens /settings/users/invite, picks email + role + optional team. Server Action calls invite_user RPC (SECURITY DEFINER, runs as service-role, validates auth.uid() is admin/GM of tenant_id from JWT).
2) RPC inserts into public.invitations: {id uuid, tenant_id, email (lowercased), role, invited_by, token_hash (sha256 of a 32-byte random secret; raw token never stored), expires_at = now()+72h, status='pending', created_at}. UNIQUE (tenant_id, lower(email)) where status='pending' prevents duplicates.
3) Edge Function send_invite_email (service-role, rate-limited) renders bilingual AR/EN email via Resend or SES-Frankfurt. Email contains: firm name, inviter name, role, one button -> https://{tenant-slug}.qaf.sa/invite/accept?token={raw_token}&id={invitation_id}. Token is the only secret; id is a lookup index. No PII in URL fragment.
4) User clicks. Public route /invite/accept hashes the raw token and looks up invitation by (id, token_hash, status='pending', expires_at>now()). On miss -> generic "expired or already used" (no info leak).
5) Branch A — invitee has NO auth.users account: redirected to /invite/accept/signup, enters password + name. supabase.auth.signUp with email_confirm=true short-circuit (because invite = email proof). On success, atomic RPC redeem_invitation: (a) creates tenant_users row {tenant_id, user_id, role, status='active', joined_at, invited_by}, (b) marks invitation status='accepted', redeemed_at, redeemed_by, (c) if role in ('admin','partner') sets a server-side flag mfa_enrollment_required=true on the user. User is then redirected to /mfa/enroll before any other page.
6) Branch B — invitee ALREADY has auth.users (different firm? deactivated previous account? same email reused?): we DENY at redeem_invitation. Rule: one human = one active tenant_users row. If the existing user has any active tenant_users row (any tenant), redemption fails with code TENANT_CONFLICT and the admin is notified. The invitee must leave the other firm first. (See edge_cases.)
7) Refresh: after redeem_invitation the access token is force-refreshed (supabase.auth.refreshSession) so custom_access_token_hook re-runs and emits tenant_id + role.
8) Cancellation: admin can revoke invitation while status='pending' — sets status='revoked'. Token hash is kept (not the raw) for audit.
9) Audit: every step (invited, sent, opened, accepted, revoked, expired) writes to audit_log with actor, tenant_id, target_email_hash (sha256 + tenant-scoped salt, NOT raw email, for GDPR).
10) Rate limits: max 50 pending invitations per tenant; max 10 invitations per inviter per hour. Enforced in the RPC via a CTE count check before insert.
authModel.mfa_strategy: TOTP only (RFC 6238, 30s, SHA1, 6 digits) via Supabase Auth /mfa/enroll endpoint — no SMS (Saudi telco delivery + cost), no WebAuthn in v1 (Phase 2). Enrollment yields a secret + QR for Google Authenticator / Microsoft Authenticator / 1Password. Verification challenge upgrades aal1 → aal2 for the session. REQUIRED for roles admin, partner. OPTIONAL but encouraged for general_manager, executive_director, manager, accountant, auditor — UI shows a banner; not enforced server-side in v1 (configurable per tenant in v1.1 via tenants.mfa_required_roles jsonb). NOT applicable to lawyer, consultant, marketer, secretary unless tenant opts in. Enforcement: (a) middleware.ts checks role + aal on every request; required-role users with aal=aal1 are redirected to /mfa/challenge on every protected route, (b) sensitive RLS policies (financial, audit, tenant_users management) include AND (auth.jwt()->>'aal') = 'aal2' so even a bug in middleware can't leak data. Recovery codes: 10 single-use codes generated at enrollment, shown once, hash-stored (bcrypt) in auth.mfa_factors metadata. Lost-device flow: user enters a recovery code on /mfa/recover; on success the factor is removed and user must re-enroll within 24h or account locks. Owner forgets MFA — see edge_cases. Service-role NEVER bypasses MFA from a client; only an out-of-band identity-verified support flow (manual ticket, ID document) can reset the last MFA factor on an admin account, and only via a logged Edge Function with two-person approval recorded in audit_log.

edge_cases.case: User invited to a second firm while already active in firm A (multi-tenancy NO).
edge_cases.handling: redeem_invitation RPC rejects with TENANT_CONFLICT. UNIQUE partial index: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX one_active_membership ON tenant_users (user_id) WHERE status='active' AND revoked_at IS NULL. User must be revoked from firm A first; admin of firm B sees a clear error 'this email is already a member of another firm on قاف'. No info about WHICH firm leaks.

edge_cases.case: User removed (revoked) mid-session — still holds a valid 1h access token.
edge_cases.handling: Revocation RPC: (1) UPDATE tenant_users SET status='revoked', revoked_at=now(), revoked_by=auth.uid() WHERE id=$1; (2) bump tenant_users.membership_version; (3) INSERT INTO auth.refresh_token_blacklist (user_id, valid_after=now()) so all refresh tokens fail; (4) call supabase.auth.admin.signOut(user_id, scope='global') via service-role to invalidate all refresh tokens server-side. Access token remains usable up to ~1h BUT custom_access_token_hook ran on issue, so its tenant_id and role are stale. Defense: every RLS policy ALSO joins to tenant_users live to check status='active' for write operations on critical tables — single extra index lookup, ~0.1ms. For reads, we accept up to 60min staleness as the documented tradeoff (called out in security doc). High-risk tables (finance, audit, user management) use the live join even for reads.

edge_cases.case: Password reset — could it cross tenants?
edge_cases.handling: Reset flow is global at auth.users level (one email = one password = one human across قاف). Reset email goes to the email's verified address, not tenant-scoped. Reset DOES NOT change tenant_users rows or roles. After reset, the user logs in on whichever tenant.qaf.sa they belong to; their existing tenant_users row drives the new JWT. If user has zero active memberships post-reset, login succeeds but tenant_id is null in JWT, all tenant tables RLS-deny, and they land on /no-tenant page. Reset emails are sent from auth@qaf.sa (not tenant subdomain) to make this clear.

edge_cases.case: Tenant suspended (non-payment / TOS violation / Moyasar chargeback / admin request).
edge_cases.handling: tenants.status -> 'suspended'. Two-layer enforcement: (1) custom_access_token_hook emits tenant_status='suspended' on the very next refresh (≤1h); (2) global RLS guard policy on EVERY tenant table: USING ((auth.jwt()->>'tenant_status') IN ('active','trial','past_due')). past_due = read-only (separate WITH CHECK on writes denies). suspended = no read, no write. Middleware additionally redirects any logged-in user on a suspended tenant to /suspended (showing billing contact + Moyasar reactivation link). admin role is allowed read access to /settings/billing ONLY (route allowlist) so they can pay and self-restore. Edge Function moyasar_webhook flips status back to 'active' on successful charge and bumps membership_version so JWTs refresh.

edge_cases.case: Owner / sole admin forgets MFA and has no recovery codes.
edge_cases.handling: No self-service reset (would defeat MFA). Out-of-band recovery: (1) user opens support ticket from a previously-verified secondary email recorded at tenant creation (mandatory field), (2) submits photo of national ID + selfie + tenant CR (Commercial Registration), (3) قاف support runs a 2-person-approval Edge Function reset_owner_mfa(user_id, ticket_id, approver_2_id) — service-role, audited, both approvers must be platform admins, (4) function removes the mfa_factor, forces password reset, and inserts an audit_log entry visible to ALL admins of the tenant. 48h hard SLA. If user is the SOLE admin and cannot prove identity, account stays locked — tenant can either appoint a new admin via legal documentation (CR change) or wait for GDPR-erasure / data-export request.

edge_cases.case: User belongs to firm A, fired, re-hired 6 months later by firm A.
edge_cases.handling: Old tenant_users row stays with status='revoked'. New invitation creates a NEW tenant_users row (different id, new joined_at). Historical FKs (created_by on cases, documents) point to the user_id which is unchanged, so attribution survives. RLS uses the ACTIVE row's role for current permissions.
Supabase Auth confirm-email flow. Email is auth-layer only; tenant_users does NOT store email. No tenant-side reconciliation needed. Audit log records old_email_hash -> new_email_hash.

edge_cases.case: Admin tries to demote themselves to non-admin while being the only admin.
edge_cases.handling: RPC change_role checks: IF role_to=anything-not-admin AND target=auth.uid() AND (SELECT count(*) FROM tenant_users WHERE tenant_id=$1 AND role='admin' AND status='active')=1 THEN RAISE 'LAST_ADMIN'. UI shows 'invite another admin first'.

Service-role key is ONLY used server-side in five places: (1) custom_access_token_hook (runs in Postgres, not exposed), (2) Edge Function moyasar_webhook (validates Moyasar HMAC signature first), (3) Edge Function send_invite_email (rate-limited, no DB writes — just reads invitation by id+hash), (4) Edge Function reset_owner_mfa (2-person-approval, audited), (5) nightly cron cleanup_expired_invitations. NEVER shipped to the browser. NEVER used to bypass RLS in normal request flow — all user requests go through the authenticated client with their JWT. Stored in Supabase Vault + injected as env var; rotated quarterly.

edge_cases.case: User session active when their role is changed (e.g., lawyer -> manager).
edge_cases.handling: membership_version bumped. JWT still has old role until refresh (≤1h). For privilege-escalation-sensitive actions (approvals, billing) the server action re-fetches live role from tenant_users before executing. For privilege-de-escalation (lawyer -> consultant) we accept the ≤1h window since RLS still bounds them to their tenant. To force immediate downgrade, admin clicks 'sign out user' which calls supabase.auth.admin.signOut(user_id, scope='global').

edge_cases.case: Concurrent invitations to the same email by two admins.
edge_cases.handling: UNIQUE partial index on invitations (tenant_id, lower(email)) WHERE status='pending' makes the second INSERT fail; UI shows 'invitation already pending, sent by {inviter}'.

edge_cases.case: User on tenant A subdomain pastes a tenant B URL.
edge_cases.handling: middleware compares URL subdomain → tenant_id with jwt.tenant_id. Mismatch returns 404 (not 403) to avoid revealing tenant existence. Logged in audit_log as suspected_cross_tenant_attempt for the user's own tenant.

edge_cases.case: GDPR hard-delete request from a former employee.
edge_cases.handling: Out-of-scope of normal auth flow but supported: Edge Function gdpr_erase(user_id, requestor_evidence) anonymizes auth.users (email -> erased+{uuid}@qaf.invalid, name -> 'Former User'), keeps user_id FK intact for legal-evidence trail, soft-deletes tenant_users to revoked. Audit log retains hashes only. EU residency satisfied because Supabase project is Frankfurt.

channels.name: tenant:{tenant_id}:notifications
channels.purpose: Tenant-wide broadcasts: system announcements, billing alerts (trial expiry, payment failed, addon activation), platform-admin messages. Filtered server-side by tenant_id; RLS enforces user can only see notifications.recipient_id = auth.uid() OR recipient_role IN user_roles.

channels.name: user:{user_id}:inbox
channels.purpose: User-private push: new task assigned, memo routed to me for action, @mention in case note, hearing reminder. Channel auth-gated to auth.uid() = user_id. Highest-frequency channel — drives unread badge counts in nav.

channels.name: tenant:{tenant_id}:case:{case_id}
channels.scope: per-case (dynamic, subscribed on case page open)
channels.purpose: Live case workspace: lawyer A adds note, lawyer B sees it appear without refresh. Used on case detail page only. Auto-unsubscribed on page exit. RLS double-checks tenant_id AND case access (case_team membership or role >= partner).

channels.name: tenant:{tenant_id}:memo:{memo_id}
channels.purpose: Memo approval workflow: status transitions (draft->review->approved->signed), approver actions, inline comments. Critical for the legal-memo collaboration loop. Subscribed only while memo detail page is open.

channels.name: tenant:{tenant_id}:tasks:{assignee_user_id}
channels.purpose: Drives 'My Tasks' live counter and Kanban board. Listens for tasks where assignee_id = auth.uid(). Updates badge when a manager reassigns, status flips, due date changes.

channels.name: tenant:{tenant_id}:presence:case:{case_id}
channels.purpose: Presence-only (no DB subscription): shows 'Sarah is viewing this case' avatars on case detail page. Tracks active viewers + cursor position in shared document editor (Documents module). Auto-clears on disconnect.

channels.name: tenant:{tenant_id}:presence:memo:{memo_id}
channels.purpose: Presence-only: 'Ahmed is editing' indicator on memo collaborative edit. Prevents two approvers from acting simultaneously. Releases lock on disconnect.

channels.name: tenant:{tenant_id}:requests:queue
channels.purpose: Live intake queue for secretary/admin role: new client request submitted via portal appears instantly in dashboard. RLS restricts subscription to roles IN (secretary, admin, manager, executive_director).

channels.name: admin:platform:billing
channels.purpose: Platform admin live ops dashboard: new tenant signup, Moyasar webhook arrived, subscription state change. NOT exposed to any tenant. auth-gated to platform_admin role with no tenant_id.

channels.name: user:{user_id}:typing:{thread_id}
channels.purpose: Broadcast-only: 'X is typing...' in memo comments and case-note threads. Ephemeral, no DB write. Throttled client-side to 1 event / 2s.

broadcast_use_cases: Typing indicators in memo comment threads and case-note discussions (ephemeral, no persistence needed; throttled to one event per 2 seconds).
Optimistic UI cursor sync in collaborative memo editor (broadcast cursor position + selection range; never written to DB).

Toast notifications triggered by another user's action in the same case context (e.g., 'Sarah just approved this memo') — fired alongside DB change for immediate UI feedback even before postgres_changes propagates.
Platform-admin-to-tenant emergency announcements (e.g., maintenance window in 10 minutes) — broadcast to tenant:{tenant_id}:notifications without inserting per-user notification rows.
Live document-editor operational transforms / CRDT ops for concurrent memo editing (high-frequency, ephemeral; DB only stores the final autosave snapshot every 30s).
presence_use_cases: Case viewer awareness: avatar stack on case detail page showing 'Sarah, Khalid are also viewing' — prevents duplicate work and signals coordination opportunity.
Memo edit lock indicator: when a partner opens a memo for approval, other approvers see 'Ahmed is reviewing' to avoid simultaneous conflicting actions.
Document collaborative editing: live cursors + selections in shared legal-document editor (Documents module) — Google-Docs-style multi-user awareness.
Online status in user directory: green dot next to lawyers currently online — used by secretary/manager when assigning urgent tasks or routing client calls.
Hearing-room presence (future): when a court hearing is in progress, lawyers attending mark themselves present so back-office sees live status without phone calls.
pubsub_use_cases: postgres_changes on notifications table filtered by recipient_id = auth.uid() — drives the universal bell-icon unread counter; INSERT events only.
postgres_changes on tasks table filtered by assignee_id = auth.uid() OR created_by = auth.uid() — live Kanban + 'My Tasks' counter; INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE.
postgres_changes on memo_workflow_status filtered by memo_id IN (memos I authored OR am an approver on) — drives status pills on memo list and detail page.
postgres_changes on case_timeline_events filtered by case_id = current case — live timeline feed on case detail page (notes added, status changed, document attached, hearing scheduled).
postgres_changes on case_documents filtered by case_id = current case — instantly shows newly uploaded files in the case Documents tab.
postgres_changes on memo_comments filtered by memo_id = current memo — live comment thread on memo review page.
postgres_changes on client_requests with INSERT filter — secretary/admin intake queue dashboard for newly submitted client requests via tenant portal.
postgres_changes on mentions table filtered by mentioned_user_id = auth.uid() — drives @mention notifications in case notes and memo comments.
postgres_changes on tenant_subscriptions (platform-admin only, no tenant filter) — operational dashboard at admin.qaf.sa showing payment events as Moyasar webhooks land.
cost_concerns: Supabase Pro plan caps: 500 concurrent realtime connections and 2 million messages/month included. With 11 roles per firm and ~30-100 users per mid-tier tenant on the 499 SAR plan, a single active tenant can use 30-80 concurrent connections. The 500-conn limit is hit at roughly 5-15 active tenants — must monitor and upgrade or shard early.

postgres_changes on per-case and per-memo channels MUST be scoped to currently-open page only. If we leave these subscribed globally (e.g., to power 'recent activity' sidebar), every tenant write fans out to every user — message count explodes 10-100x. Enforce subscribe-on-mount / unsubscribe-on-unmount discipline in the frontend.

RLS-filtered realtime is expensive: Supabase re-runs the RLS policy on every change to decide who receives it. Heavy joins in policies (e.g., case_team membership check via subquery) slow down the realtime worker. Use SECURITY DEFINER helper functions with STABLE marking, and consider denormalized tenant_id + assignee_id columns on every realtime table to keep policies index-only.

Broadcast channels (typing, cursors) are cheap per-event but high-frequency. A 5-person collaborative memo edit can emit 50 messages/second per user. MUST throttle client-side (typing: max 1/2s, cursors: max 10/s) — otherwise a single editing session can consume tens of thousands of messages per hour.

Presence has a hidden cost: every join/leave/track-update is a billable message, and presence state syncs to all subscribers on every change. On a busy case channel (20 viewers) a single user toggling tabs can emit 40+ messages. Use small presence keys (just user_id + name) and avoid storing rich state.

notifications table is the hottest realtime target — every user subscribes to it on every page. Index on (tenant_id, recipient_id, created_at DESC) is mandatory. Consider partitioning by tenant_id once we cross ~50 tenants to keep RLS evaluation fast.

Per-tenant filtering MUST happen at realtime subscription level (filter='tenant_id=eq.{tenant_id}') AND in RLS — never rely on RLS alone, because Supabase realtime still serializes the row before discarding, wasting CPU and message quota.

Platform-admin channel (admin:platform:billing) is unfiltered across tenants — keep it strictly server-evaluated to a single admin role to avoid accidental cross-tenant data leak via misconfigured client.
Avoid realtime on financial/invoice/payment tables entirely — these are low-frequency, security-sensitive, and easily polled. Same for audit_log: write-only, never realtime (also a leak risk).
Plan a circuit breaker: if Supabase reports >80% of monthly message quota by day 20, automatically degrade per-case/per-memo channels to 10s polling and keep only notifications + tasks live.
